name: Functions and Graphs
whatIsIt: A FUNCTION is a rule that turns each input into ONE output — a machine: put in x, get out f(x). f(x) = 2x + 1 means 'double it and add one'. The set of allowed inputs is the DOMAIN; the outputs form the RANGE. Plotting the (input, output) pairs draws the GRAPH: a straight LINE for linear functions, a U-shaped PARABOLA for quadratics.
whyNeeded: Functions describe how one thing depends on another — cost on quantity, distance on time, height on age. Reading and drawing their graphs is central to science, economics, data and all higher maths.
realLifeUses: Distance travelled as a function of time
A ball's height over time (a parabola)
Reading a graph to predict a value

vocabulary.meaning: A rule giving one output for each input x.
Allowed inputs (domain) and the outputs they give (range).
A straight-line graph (linear) or U-shaped curve (quadratic).

story.title: The Function Machine
story.text: Miss Rao draws a machine: f(x) = 2x + 1. 'Feed in 3,' she says. Ved computes f(3) = 2×3 + 1 = 7. 'Feed in 0?' f(0) = 1. He plots (3, 7) and (0, 1) and joins them — a straight line. 'Every input gives exactly one output,' says Miss Rao. 'That's what makes it a function.'

visual.caption: A function pairs each input with one output. f(x) = 2x+1 gives 1, 3, 5, 7 — plot them for a straight line.

teachingGallery.title: Rule, table, graph
teachingGallery.note: Build a table of (x, f(x)), then plot the points.

examples.caption: Linear: equal steps up → straight line.

standardMethod.summary: Apply the rule to inputs to get outputs; make a table of (x, f(x)); plot the points and join — a line for linear, a parabola for quadratic.
steps: A function gives ONE output per input: f(x) is the rule.
Substitute inputs into the rule to get outputs (f(3) = 2·3+1 = 7).
Make a table of (x, f(x)) pairs.
Plot the points (x across, f(x) up) and join: straight line for linear (2x+1), U-shaped parabola for quadratic (x²).

workedExamples.problem: For f(x) = x², find f(3) and f(−3).
steps: Both give 9 — the parabola is symmetric.

commonMistakes.mistake: Reading f(3) as f times 3 instead of putting 3 into the rule.
commonMistakes.fix: f(3) means the OUTPUT when the input is 3 — substitute 3 into the rule, don't multiply f by 3.

commonMistakes.mistake: Expecting a straight line for a quadratic like x².
commonMistakes.fix: Only linear rules (like 2x+1) give straight lines. An x² rule gives a U-shaped PARABOLA.

easy.q: For f(x) = x + 3, find f(4). (write like 7)

medium.q: For f(x) = x², find f(4). (write like 16)

challenge.q: The graph of f(x) = x² is…

questions.q: For f(x) = x + 5, find f(0). (write like 5)
For f(x) = x², find f(5). (write like 25)
The graph of a LINEAR function like 2x + 1 is…

teachBackPrompt: Draw a 'function machine' for a grown-up (like f(x) = 2x + 1). Feed in a few inputs, list the outputs in a table, plot the points on a grid, and show how linear rules make a straight line while x² makes a U-shaped parabola.

revisionCard.summary: Function = a rule giving ONE output per input: f(x). Domain = inputs, range = outputs. Find f(3) by substituting 3 into the rule. Make a table of (x, f(x)), plot and join: linear (2x+1) → straight line; quadratic (x²) → U-shaped parabola.
